case_title: LANCE FARRELL VS. RYSIGO TECHNOLOGIES CORP. et al

docket_entries.description: COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT FARRELL, LANCE

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT LANCE FARRELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS RYSIGO TECHNOLOGIES' AND SUHAIL MAQSOOD'S CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM IN THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION AS THE DEMURRER HAS BEEN OVERRULED, AND DENIED AS MOOT AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IN LIGHT OF THE SUSTAINING OF THE DEMURRER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT LANCE FARRELL'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS RYSIGO TECHNOLOGIES' AND SUHAIL MAQSOOD'S CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE SECOND AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND OVERRULED ON ALL OTHER GROUNDS. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD THE SLANDEROUS STATEMENTS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HAVE LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN IN GOOD FAITH, TO PLEAD THE DUTY THAT IS ASSUMED BY PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT FARRELL, IMPOSED ON MR. FARRELL AS A MATTER OF LAW, OR THAT ARISES OUT OF A RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN MR. FARRELL AND MR. MAQSOOD. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RYSIGO TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF AND FOR SANCTIONS - THE COURT HAS REVIEWED ALL PAPERS FILED IN SUPPORT AND OPPOSITION TO THE MOTIONS, INCLUDING THE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS FILED BOTH PARTIES ON OCTOBER 22, 2013. SINCE THIS MATTER WAS HEARD BY THE PRO TEM JUDGE ON SEPTEMBER 16, 2013, PLAINTIFF HAS ATTENDED HIS DEPOSITION. THUS, THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS NOW MOOT. AS TO SANCTIONS, THE COURT FINDS THAT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION DID NOT EXIST FOR PLAINTIFF UNILATERALLY CONTINUING THE DEPOSITION A FEW DAYS BEFORE IT WAS SCHEDULED TO OCCUR ON AUGUST 8 AND 9, 2013. (CCP 2025.450(G)(1).) ACCORDINGLY, SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,822.90 WILL BE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF RYSIGO TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION AND PAYABLE BY PLAINTIFF FARRELL WITHIN 30 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. THIS DOLLAR AMOUNT REPRESENTS DEFENDANTS' ATTORNEY FEES FOR BRINGING THIS MOTION. SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ARE DENIED BECAUSE IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION, RYSIGO TECHNOLOGIES DID NOT EXPLICITLY REQUEST SANCTIONS AGAINST COUNSEL. "A REQUEST FOR A SANCTION SHALL, IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION, IDENTIFY EVERY PERSON, PARTY AND ATTORNEY AGAINST WHOM THE SANCTION IS SOUGHT . '" (CCP 2023.040.) THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS FOR THE LOST REVENUES OF MR. MAQSOOD, CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER OF RYSIGO TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SUZANNE CROZIER, CSR # 4761 (302/MJM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-09-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Rysigo Technologies Corporation Motion To Compel Deposition
Off Calendar Oct-29-2013 Unknown
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Rysigo Technologies Corporation's Motion To Compel Deposition of Plaintiff and for sanctions - The Court has reviewed all papers filed in support and opposition to the motions, including the supplemental briefs filed both parties on October 22, 2013. Since this matter was heard by the Pro Tem Judge on September 16, 2013, Plaintiff has attended his deposition. Thus, the motion to compel is now moot. As to sanctions, the Court finds that substantial justification did not exist for Plaintiff unilaterally continuing the deposition a few days before it was scheduled to occur on August 8 and 9, 2013. (CCP 2025.450(g)(1).) Accordingly, sanctions in the amount of $2,822.90 will be awarded in favor of Rysigo Technologies Corporation and payable by Plaintiff Farrell within 30 days of entry of this order. This dollar amount represents defendants' attorney fees for bringing this motion. Sanctions against Plaintiff's counsel are denied because in the Notice of Motion, Rysigo Technologies did not explicitly request sanctions against counsel. "A request for a sanction shall, in the notice of motion, identify every person, party and attorney against whom the sanction is sought . '" (CCP 2023.040.) The request for sanctions for the lost revenues of Mr. Maqsood, Chief Operating Officer of Rysigo Technologies Corporation is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Suzanne Crozier, CSR # 4761 =(302/MJM)

Off Calendar Jun-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT LANCE FARRELL's DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS RYSIGO TECHNOLOGIES' AND SUHAIL MAQSOOD'S CROSS-COMPLAINT is Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the second and fourth causes of action, and overruled on all other grounds. Cross-Complainants are granted leave to amend to add the slanderous statements to second cause of action. As to the Fourth cause of action, Cross-Complainants have leave to amend, if they can in good faith, to plead the duty that is assumed by Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Farrell, imposed on Mr. Farrell as a matter of law, or that arises out of a relationship between Mr. Farrell and Mr. Maqsood.
